ogTitle: Pitnacree vs Pitt Town Bottoms: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pitnacree and Pitt Town Bottoms.
heading: Pitnacree vs Pitt Town Bottoms — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pitnacree, New South Wales and Pitt Town Bottoms, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pitt Town Bottoms has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Pitnacree (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pitnacree or Pitt Town Bottoms safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pitnacree has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Pitt Town Bottoms scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pitnacree scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. Pitt Town Bottoms scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pitnacree or Pitt Town Bottoms?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pitt Town Bottoms has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
